description:
Step 1 of fz-sj6 landed (exact-dedup at dnf_union, idempotence
encoded). Step 2 (subsumption-based dedup: drop clauses subsumed
by others) was queued but no current golden exercises a
subsumable-clause pattern (`list(int) ∨ list(int|float)` etc.).

Land this when a real program / fixture produces specs with
subsumable clauses and we want them collapsed in the goldens.

## Algorithm

For each pair (Cᵢ, Cⱼ) of clauses in the deduped Vec, check
whether the single-clause Descr formed by Cᵢ on its axis is a
subtype of the single-clause Descr formed by Cⱼ. If yes, drop
Cᵢ. O(n²) per union — n is small post-dedup.

## Soundness

`A ⊆ B ⇒ A ∨ B = B`. Absorption.

## Out of scope

Mass-merge (`list(A) ∨ list(B) → list(A∨B)`) — unsound for
heterogeneous lists.
title: fz-dnf-union-subsumption: drop clauses subsumed by others
